---
title: "Alert"
description: "Displays a callout for user attention."
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
library(shiny)
library(shinyblocks)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Alert

Displays a callout for user attention.

## Usage

```r
library(shinyblocks)

block_alert(
  block_alert_title("Heads up!"),
  block_alert_description("You can add components to your app using the cli.")
)
```

## Examples

### Basic

A standard alert with an icon and description.

<div class="sb-component-preview">
```{r, echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
cat(as.character(block_alert(
  block_icon("terminal", class = "h-4 w-4"),
  block_alert_title("Heads up!"),
  block_alert_description("You can add components to your app using the cli.")
)))
```
</div>
<div class="sb-component-code-title">Code</div>
```r
block_alert(
  block_icon("terminal", class = "h-4 w-4"),
  block_alert_title("Heads up!"),
  block_alert_description("You can add components to your app using the cli.")
)
```

### Variants

Use the `variant` prop to change the visual style of the alert.

<div class="sb-component-preview">
```{r, echo=FALSE, results="asis"}
cat(as.character(block_alert(
  variant = "destructive",
  block_icon("alert-circle", class = "h-4 w-4"),
  block_alert_title("Error"),
  block_alert_description("Your session has expired. Please log in again.")
)))
```
</div>
<div class="sb-component-code-title">Code</div>
```r
block_alert(
  variant = "destructive",
  block_icon("alert-circle", class = "h-4 w-4"),
  block_alert_title("Error"),
  block_alert_description("Your session has expired. Please log in again.")
)
```
